description_long: This gatehouse features a robust arched entryway framed by large, angular stone blocks that give it a fortified appearance. The central door is constructed from vertically aligned wooden planks, reinforced with two prominent horizontal iron bands and a round iron handle. The stonework is irregular, with visible cracks and chiseled textures, suggesting age and durability. The arch above the door is capped with a keystone, enhancing the medieval aesthetic. The rear side of the structure mirrors the front but lacks the iron reinforcements and handle, exposing the wooden planks more clearly. The overall design is compact and defensive, suitable for controlling access to a walled area or courtyard.
description: A fortified arched gatehouse with heavy stone framing and a reinforced wooden door, featuring iron bands and a handle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the stone frame and the layered construction of the gatehouse.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the arched stone entry, wooden door with iron bands, and a round handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the depth and solidity of the structure.
description_view_3: Rear view revealing the wooden planks of the door without iron reinforcements or handle.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone doorway with a single reinforced wooden door and iron bands.
attribute_window_placement: No windows visible on the structure.
